name: Bobby Plater
abstract: Robert C. Plater (May 13, 1914, Newark, New Jersey – November 20, 1982, Lake Tahoe) was an American jazz alto saxophonist.

concerts.label: Billy Eckstine with Count Basie and His Orchestra
Kool Jazz Festival: Ella Fitzgerald and Count Basie
Kool Jazz Festival: Ella Fitzgerald and Count Basie

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: Bobby Plater came up to me one time we were playing... He said, 'you can’t do that. We’ll come back in right, we’ll get out just in time.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: the reed section was Bobby Plater... Anyhow, the rhythm section was Elmer Gill... and I later worked with him for a spell... The band...

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: No, there was an alto saxophone player named Bobby Plater. He was the lead alto player... Lionel, like I said, he was a great player... We were playing at a place that used to be called the Band Box... Lionel, he was coming down to get him next week... both of them played.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: I was quite amazed how some of the guys in the band, like saxophonist Bobby Plater... Bobby Plater was always telling me... my first appreciation of getting to know the guys who were much older than me
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Dodgion
about_quotes.evidence: he said Bobby Plater had to take off... so would I come in and play. I said great. So I got to play with Marshall Royal, with Basie
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Dodgion interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Gus Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Well, I remember guys like Bobby Plater saying to me... [INTERVIEWEE] Oh, yeah, he would do that, he would do that.
